Monday, March 9, 2026 Daily Digest

Karpathy's Autoresearch Runs 100 Experiments Overnight, Pro-Human Declaration Sets AI Guardrails, New Chip Export Rules Coming

Today's top stories in AI + enterprise tech

1

Karpathy Releases Autoresearch: Autonomous AI Agents That Never Stop

Andrej Karpathy — former Tesla AI director and OpenAI co-founder — just published autoresearch on GitHub: an open-source framework where AI agents autonomously run 100+ ML experiments overnight on a single GPU. The agent modifies code, trains, checks results, and iterates — no human in the loop. The repo hit 8,000 stars in days. The enterprise takeaway: "agentic" has moved from research concept to production pattern, and the human role is shifting from doing to reviewing.

Read on LeapLytics →
2

The Pro-Human Declaration: A Roadmap for AI (If Anyone Will Listen)

A bipartisan coalition has published the Pro-Human Declaration — a framework for responsible AI development that emerged amid the Pentagon-Anthropic standoff. Key provisions: prohibition on superintelligence development until there's scientific consensus it can be done safely, mandatory off-switches on powerful systems, and a ban on architectures capable of self-replication or autonomous self-improvement. Polling shows 95% of Americans oppose an unregulated race to superintelligence.

Read on TechCrunch →
3

White House Drafting New AI Chip Export Rules

The administration is drafting regulations that would tighten US oversight of global AI chip exports. Foreign nations seeking advanced chips may need to make "matching" investments in American AI infrastructure or provide government-to-government security assurances. This reshapes how compute power gets distributed globally — and could affect enterprise AI roadmaps for anyone depending on non-US compute.

Read on DigiTimes →
4

AI Marketing Analytics Platforms: 2026 Guide

Comprehensive breakdown of what's working in AI-powered marketing analytics. The key insight: AI-powered anomaly detection now continuously monitors for unusual patterns — sudden drops in conversion rates, unexpected spikes in CPA, performance deviations — and alerts you before they impact results. The platforms that matter have 500+ native connectors eliminating manual data collection.

Read on Cometly →
5

How AI Tools Help SMBs Automate Marketing in 2026

The shift from traditional SEO to AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) is real: if your business isn't optimized for AI voices and chatbots, you're invisible to Alexa, Siri, and ChatGPT. The stat that matters: 85% of marketers save at least 4 hours per week using AI. AI tools now audit websites for "AI readability" and fix technical issues so that ChatGPT and Gemini recognize your brand as a trusted source.

Read on Business To Mark →

💡 My Take

Read this one: The Karpathy autoresearch piece. The design philosophy — autonomous, self-directed, metric-driven iteration with an explicit instruction to never stop and never ask permission — is the template enterprise AI is moving toward. Not just for ML research, but for any domain with clear objectives and measurable output. The question for every enterprise team: what's your "val_bpb"?
